cognitive_redirect.name: cognitive_redirect
cognitive_redirect.purpose: Real-time detection of Claude's documented failure-mode language (e.g. "let me just", "I'll work around", "this should be fine") with a SystemReminder that fires on the NEXT turn. Pulls Claude back to the PACT-correct path before the wrong action ships. Brag-citation system appends a track-record line when Claude has heeded the same pattern with high precision before — recognition that earns trust.
triggers: {'event': 'UserPromptSubmit', 'handler': 'cognitive-redirect.sh', 'consequence': 'Scans the prompt; if a pattern matches, queues a SystemReminder for next turn'}
{'event': 'Stop (turn end)', 'handler': 'cognitive-outcome-tag.sh', 'consequence': 'Tags whether the previous redirect was heeded or ignored — feeds learning loop'}
invariants: Pattern matching is regex-based — fast (<5ms) and deterministic
Only triggers with weighted precision >= 0.6 are active by default
Brag citations append, never replace, the redirect message
Outcome tagging closes the loop: heeded vs ignored feeds trigger weights
Learn-triggers job runs on a cadence — promotes/demotes patterns based on observed outcomes
author_notes: Behavior of the EmotionPrompt-backed cognitive-redirection system: how
Claude's user-prompt text is scanned for failure-mode patterns, how
matching patterns inject SystemReminders, and how outcomes feed the

dashboard_yaml_sync.name: dashboard_yaml_sync
dashboard_yaml_sync.purpose: Wave-end gate that prevents the assistant from ending a response when dashboard.html has been edited but dashboard_build.yaml hasn't been updated to log the work. Soft nudge during the wave; hard block at the Stop hook. Ensures every visible feature change has a corresponding audit-log entry that drives the project's XP economy.
triggers: {'event': 'PostToolUse on Edit/Write to pact-dashboard.html', 'handler': 'post-edit-dashboard-flag.sh', 'consequence': 'Touches the flag file'}
{'event': 'PostToolUse on Edit/Write to dashboard_build.yaml', 'handler': 'post-edit-dashboard-flag.sh', 'consequence': 'Removes the flag file'}
invariants: PostToolUse on dashboard.html sets a session-scoped flag
PostToolUse on dashboard_build.yaml CLEARS that flag
Stop hook blocks turn-end if flag is still set
Soft reminder during the wave (followup hook) — non-blocking
Block message points the assistant directly at dashboard_build.yaml with the exact YAML stub to add
author_notes: How the dashboard.html (codebase) and dashboard_build.yaml (audit log +
XP source) stay in lockstep. Without this gate, you can ship HTML
without logging — which breaks both the audit trail and the XP banner.

repo_map_pipeline.name: repo_map_pipeline
repo_map_pipeline.purpose: Aider-style symbol index over the codebase, auto-rebuilt in real time as files change. Replaces grep-spawning by giving Claude a single JSON oracle for "what symbols exist, who calls whom, what flows does this file participate in." The dashboard's Repo Map tab reads the same JSON.
triggers: {'event': 'PostToolUse on Edit/Write to *.py / *.dart / *.ts / feature_flows/*.yaml', 'handler': 'post-edit-repo-map-dirty.sh', 'consequence': 'Marks build dirty + spawns builder if no lock held'}
{'event': 'Manual: python scripts/repo_map.py build', 'handler': 'repo_map.py', 'consequence': 'Synchronous full rebuild; logs to history JSONL'}
invariants: Every Edit/Write to a tracked source file marks the build dirty
Concurrent edits collapse into ONE tail rebuild via loop-while-dirty pattern
Only one builder runs at a time — PID-tracked lockfile prevents concurrent builds
History is JSONL append-only; identical consecutive lines are deduped
repo_map.json is the single source of truth for the dashboard's Repo Map tab
Dashboard polls the JSON via fetch — no IPC, no websocket, no shared memory
author_notes: DO NOT duplicate wiring here. repo_map.json owns structure (symbol index,
imports, PageRank, drift). This file owns BEHAVIOR — what triggers a
rebuild, how concurrent edits collapse, how the dashboard stays current.
